target_population.text: # Cost-Effectiveness Publication Excerpt The publication reports that a partitioned-survival model was considered, but a Markov model was selected due to data availability and transparency for validation. Key assumption: treatment effect waning begins after year 3 in base case. Validation notes: internal validity checks compared model-predicted survival with trial Kaplan-Meier observations. External validity assessed against registry benchmarks. Open issue: subgroup-specific health-state utilities were unavailable and borrowed from a related population.

HTA Appraisal Summary Decision context: reimbursement decision for first-line treatment in advanced condition X. Target population: adults with confirmed diagnosis of condition X after biomarker testing. Comparators: standard chemotherapy regimen A and targeted therapy B. Perspective: national healthcare payer perspective. Time horizon: lifetime horizon is preferred to capture all survival and cost differences. Cycle length: monthly cycle selected because treatment assessments occur every 4 weeks. Model structure: state-transition Markov model with progression-free, progressed disease, and death states. Clinical inputs: overall survival and progression-free survival from randomized controlled trial data. Cost inputs: drug acquisition, administration, adverse event management, follow-up, and terminal care. Utility inputs: EQ-5D utility estimates with disutilities for grade 3+ adverse events. Uncertainty: deterministic one-way sensitivity analyses and probabilistic

care. Utility inputs: EQ-5D utility estimates with disutilities for grade 3+ adverse events. Uncertainty: deterministic one-way sensitivity analyses and probabilistic sensitivity analysis required. Known gap: immature long-term survival data, requiring extrapolation assumptions.
